name: Senator Jeff Merkley
subject: They're cowering while our country is dying
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jeff Merkley for Oregon
body:
| [](https://secure.jeffmerkley.com/page/m/16c2d912/6cd27893/501947d5/1580cda3/1082660659/VEsH/?g=fjbuEq_HJ6FcsCg1a40jRRA "http://www.jeffmerkley.com")
---

| | This weekend, we saw two mass shootings within 24 hours of each other. Like you, I've just had it. America doesn't have markedly more mental illness or poverty or more violent video games than other countries. What we do have? Way more guns. And violent white nationalism being encouraged from the White House. It's a toxic combination. Assault rifles are weapons of war. A semi-automatic rifle and high-capacity magazines are designed for carnage. The Dayton shooter killed and wounded three dozen people in only 30 seconds. We need to get these weapons off the street. We need to close the loopholes that let dangerous people arm themselves. These steps might not end all killings, but it's an NRA argument that if we can't stop all shootings, we shouldn't try to stop any. And that's really what it comes down to: Will Mitch McConnell, Donald Trump, and others in Washington ever decide that the lives of Americans take priority over the NRA? And will other Republicans ever denounce white nationalism and commit the same kind of focused effort at fighting white nationalist terrorism that they're so eager to do when the terrorists come from the Middle East? I'm tired of the empty rhetoric and hollow promises. We need leaders who will put address this national crisis, and it they're not willing to, they need to go. [**_Join me on Facebook. Say no more to this awfulness and commit to voting each and every person responsible out of office in 2020._**](https://secure.jeffmerkley.com/page/m/16c2d912/6cd27893/501947d5/1580cda0/1082660659/VEsE/?g=fjbuEq_HJ6FcsCg1a40jRRA "https://www.facebook.com/JeffMerkleyOregon/posts/2789438904408516") **_Jeff_** |

name: Wilmot Collins
subject: Endless gun violence cannot be our new normal
disclaimer_text: Paid for by A whole lot of folks for Wilmot Collins, PO Box 458, Helena, MT 59624
body:
#
Friend,
In the wake of this weekend's unimaginable tragedies, my heart aches for El Paso and Dayton.
But we all know that the standard responses from our leaders are doing nothing for the inevitable future victims of mass shootings. We must come together and fight gun violence.
**[As your next Senator, I will do more than offer my thoughts and prayers. I will work to stop the plague of gun violence in our nation. Add your name if you're with me.](https://act.wilmotcollins.com/page/m/561647b6/1e3760f0/69f4e8ee/5f7e9ec7/2074023340/VEsH/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IjE4MzI2IiwiSlNWR1NWSlRWRTVCVFVVbEpRPT0iOiIifQ==/?g=ENsuPVNw52hc6i6rfY8XtkA "https://wilmotcollins.cp.bsd.net/page/signup/end-gun-violence?source=em190805&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&firstname=")**
I lived through gun violence in the Liberian Civil War and would not wish that pain on anybody. I watched family members fall and I know that parents shouldn't fear a shooting when they send their kids to school. Weekend shoppers shouldn't have to be afraid of being gunned down in a Walmart. No one should be scared of being shot while going to the movies or attending a concert. We cannot let this become our new normal. We must act.
While Steve Daines refuses to take action to end gun violence and refuses to apologize for condoning President Trump's hateful and divisive rhetoric, I am not afraid to take on this fight. I will do everything I can to help keep families in Montana and across the nation safe from harm.
**[Add your name: It's time for strong, decisive action to end the scourge of gun violence.](https://act.wilmotcollins.com/page/m/561647b6/1e3760f0/69f4e8ee/5f7e9ec7/2074023340/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IjE4MzI2IiwiSlNWR1NWSlRWRTVCVFVVbEpRPT0iOiIifQ==/?g=ENsuPVNw52hc6i6rfY8XtkA "https://wilmotcollins.cp.bsd.net/page/signup/end-gun-violence?source=em190805&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&firstname=")**
Thank you,
Wilmot

name: Tina Smith
subject: Time to demand action
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Tina Smith for Minnesota
body:
It's time to demand action against gun violence.

| Friend,

How many senseless mass shootings will it take for Congress to value the safety of families over appeasing the NRA?

_Moms Demand Action_ is a non-profit organization that is not sitting back and waiting for an answer to that question. Thanks to grassroots support and volunteers across the country, _Moms Demand Action_ has achieved unprecedented victories against the gun lobby -- fighting for public safety measures and stronger gun laws that protect families from gun violence.

I've met with many of these moms and volunteers in town halls and events across Minnesota, and I have seen the determination in their faces to do what is right.

And if we want to prevent the kind of violence we saw in El Paso and Dayton over the weekend, then it will take folks from every walk of life with that same determination demanding action of your elected officials.

**Will you join _Moms Demand Action_ and volunteer your time to protect our communities against gun violence?**

**[Yes, I'll volunteer to end gun violence.](https://action.tinaforminnesota.com/page/m/4bc4e131/c1f7015/64dfebba/1146f280/2321521835/VEsH/?g=YCjTcDpTVzRarKyUt8_RZpA "https://momsdemandaction.org/act/")**

**[No, I can't volunteer right now, but I'll pitch in $10 to _Mom 's Demand_ to help end gun violence.](https://action.tinaforminnesota.?g=YCjTcDpTVzRarKyUt8_RZpA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/moms-demand-action/?refcode=TinaSmith&amount=10&recurring=auto&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=PA&phone=&employer=&occupation=")**

Thank you,

Tina

name: Abigail Spanberger
subject: The shootings this past weekend.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Spanberger for Congress
body:
Peter,

Over the course of 13 hours, Americans suffered through two more mass shootings. At least 31 people in Texas and Ohio are now dead, and many more lie injured.

In Dayton, police responded within one minute, a testament to the courage and decisive action of local law enforcement. Yet despite their immediate response, the shooter was still able to murder nine people.

In El Paso, the shooter authored a violent, racist manifesto--full of white supremacist ideology and blind hatred towards immigrants--before murdering 22 people who were out shopping on a Saturday morning.

[_**Back-to-back shootings like these demonstrate a fundamental failure our country faces, which is to put simple gun violence prevention laws into place.**_](https://act.myngp.com/el/tkESKQvYUP8fJBJqFO5LWB8yzOtXFlNdnpHEa_BjXGg=/k4hinlUXsXHpWKsmoYkfXTp1c4OSzON7qOCO-brrFKI=) | | | [ SIGN THE PETITION](https://act.myngp.com/el/tkESKQvYUP8fJBJqFO5LWB8yzOtXFlNdnpHEa_BjXGg=/k4hinlUXsXHpWKsmoYkfXTp1c4OSzON7qOCO-brrFKI=)
---
In the House of Representatives, we passed--with bipartisan support--a universal background check bill. The bill's premise is simple: we have background checks at all federally-licensed gun sellers, and we should apply the same standard to all firearms purchases everywhere--including online and at gun shows. We also passed an amendment to close the Charleston Loophole, which allowed a man who should not have been able to acquire a firearm to buy a gun and then walk into a church armed and murder nine people during Bible study.

In the House, we passed this legislation, and yet Senate Majority Leader Mitch McConnell will not bring our bill up for a vote. Our legislation will not fully solve the problem, but it's a critical step toward preventing the senseless loss of life we are faced with all too often. Furthermore, the vast majority of Americans support it, and yet the Senate routinely obstructs our progress.

We have no choice but to act. The victims in Dayton, El Paso, Virginia Beach, Charleston, Newtown--and in so many more places across our country too heartbreaking to list --deserve more than empty promises. They deserve action. The Senate must bring our bills to the floor for a vote, and every elected leader must not only denounce the hate that is eating away at the fabric of our communities but take action to prevent it from manifesting into yet another mass murder.

[_**Sign the petition: tell the Senate to bring our bills to a vote >>>**_](https://act.myngp.com/el/tkESKQvYUP8fJBJqFO5LWB8yzOtXFlNdnpHEa_BjXGg=/k4hinlUXsXHpWKsmoYkfXTp1c4OSzON7qOCO-brrFKI=)
| | [ SIGN THE PETITION](https://act.myngp.com/el/tkESKQvYUP8fJBJqFO5LWB8yzOtXFlNdnpHEa_BjXGg=/k4hinlUXsXHpWKsmoYkfXTp1c4OSzON7qOCO-brrFKI=)
---

_Thank you,

-Abigail_

clean_body: I want to take the opportunity one more time to ask for your vote in this primary. Everyone, I want to take the opportunity one more time to ask for your vote in this primary. Your support has been incredible so far, but the most important thing is to make sure you get out and vote before polls close this evening. Traveling to all areas of Mississippi I've seen firsthand the positive impact that conservative policies have had on our state, but I know there is more work to be done. I vow that, as your Governor, I will be a voice for you and your interests. That’s why I am asking you to make your voice heard today by casting your ballot. If you haven’t voted yet, there’s still time before the day is over. Click below to find your polling place, and remember to vote! For Mississippi, Team Tate Paid for by Tate for Governor | | | | | ---|---|---|--- Email sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com by Tate for Governor P.O. Box 24355, Jackson, MS 39225

| Friend, we're back on the ground in Iowa, ready to hit the road for our _second_ state tour in our beloved Winnebago--if you haven't seen it yet, here it is.
[](https://secure.hickenlooper.?g=Wvb-QcQot1BcsCb456pUfvA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/hickenlooper2020-130kdonors?refcode=em_fr_20190805_TW_20190805-fr-TW-iowa-all_all_i1&recurring=auto&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&city=Englewood&state=&occupation=&employer=")
We've said before that the way we prevail in this primary is to put in the time and effort on the ground in early states. We're going all out to make sure that our message gets out to every Iowa voter. [](https://secure.hickenlooper.?g=Wvb-QcQot1BcsCb456pUfvA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/hickenlooper2020-130kdonors?refcode=em_fr_20190805_TW_20190805-fr-TW-iowa-all_all_contribbutton&recurring=auto&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&city=Englewood&state=&occupation=&employer=") On the previous Winnebago tour, we rolled up in Centerville to visit Indian Hills Community College and spoke to folks over coffee in Grinnell. We toured rural health centers, met with teachers and union members--and while on the road, we found out that we were _tied in polling with candidates like Beto O'Rourke and Cory Booker._ [_**You can help us keep up the momentum by chipping in now.**_](https://secure.hickenlooper.?g=Wvb-QcQot1BcsCb456pUfvA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/hickenlooper2020-130kdonors?refcode=em_fr_20190805_TW_20190805-fr-TW-iowa-all_all_l1&recurring=auto&amount=1&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&city=Englewood&state=&occupation=&employer=")

Putting in the time pays off--and this week, that's exactly what we'll be doing. subject: President Trump Urges Nation to Condemn White Supremacy and Find Bipartisan Solutions
body:
“These sinister ideologies must be defeated. Hate has no place in America,” the President said from the White House. | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=4f77e37f39&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| | |
# **President Trump Urges Nation to Condemn White Supremacy and Find Bipartisan Solutions**

President Donald J. Trump called out the evil ideology of white supremacy yesterday and “urged the nation to condemn it with one voice in the wake of the mass shooting in El Paso, Texas,” Kathryn Watson reports for CBS News.

“These sinister ideologies must be defeated. Hate has no place in America,” the President said from the White House.

In addition to calling for bipartisan solutions to gun violence, President Trump “also directed the Justice Department to propose legislation, so that those who commit hate crimes and mass murders face the death penalty ‘quickly’ and ‘decisively,’” without years of delay.

[Click here to read more.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=18fce9a04a&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
| “The Trump administration imposed a total economic embargo against the government of Venezuela, a significant escalation of pressure against the regime of President Nicolás Maduro and countries including Russia and China that continue to support him,” Vivian Salama reports for [_The Wall Street Journal_](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=00054f0655&e=3ee4985eea). This powerful sanction is “the first action of its kind against a government in the Western Hemisphere in more than 30 years. The move places Venezuela on a par with North Korea, Iran, Syria and Cuba, the only other countries currently under such stringent U.S. measures.”
---
|
---
| The Treasury Department declared yesterday that it would designate China as a currency manipulator, following President Trump's announcement of new tariffs last week in response to China’s continuing broken promises on trade, Sean Higgins reports for the [_Washington Examiner_](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=a28ec58f1e&e=3ee4985eea). “The Chinese economy is crumbling,” White House National Economic Council Director Larry Kudlow said this morning.
---
|
---
| “Every single baby born in America deserves proper medical care and attention. That’s just common sense. Yet we continue to learn of cases — far too many — where babies are born alive during an attempted abortion, denied that crucial medical care, and left to die,” write Rep. Steve Scalise (R-LA) and Marjorie Dannenfelser, president of the pro-life Susan B. Anthony List, in [Fox News](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=91cb7921dc&e=3ee4985eea).
---

body:
|
---
| |
---
| | In April 2018, at the suggestion of several supporters, I emailed my first “500 Words”, an update of what I’m up to and thinking about, and said I’d do it again if enough people wanted me to. Scores of you responded “yes”, so I sent a second one that October, and people asked me to keep ‘em coming. No. 3 was sent in January. Here’s No. 4. [You can access previous editions here.](https://twitter.us18.list-manage.com/track/click?u=c5ac11f23df6ef9dfb9e604c5&id=b97295ddf0&e=ef726ca89e)

You are receiving this because at some point you were identified as a supporter, someone who wants to keep in touch, or you asked to be added to the list. If you know somebody who would like to receive this, have them email me at [billbryantwa@gmail.com](mailto:billbryantwa@gmail.com). If you want to unsubscribe, please click the unsubscribe link at the bottom.
---
| |
## **WHAT I'M UP TO
AND THINKING ABOUT**
---
| It’s summer! For me, that’s meant sailing, boating, paddle boarding, fishing, cycling, hiking.

I just came off a fantastic hike in the North Cascades. A climbing buddy I hadn’t seen in nine years called, said he was coming to town, wanted to get back up into the hills. “How’d nine years pass without us seeing each other, Dan?”, I asked. “Well,” he called back from in front of me on the trail, “I had three kids and you went off to save the world”. Our friendship and teamwork picked up as if our last climb had been only weeks before.
---
|
---
---
| Friends are the community we build around ourselves & our family. Through different and shared values & goals we touch each other’s lives.

My mentor told me before he died, “At the end of your life, all the stuff you’ve been able to buy won’t matter.... the only things that will matter are the lives you’ve touched and the community you’ve built.”

Friends are one of our communities.

Strengthening our state’s broader community (helping kids learn, fixing our traffic mess, protecting our public lands, watersheds, salmon runs, orca and Puget Sound, making government work better) is why I ran for governor. Doing those things requires pulling together people who have different values and goals. That’s why I’m disgusted with politicians who divide us.

What’s troublesome, is that all the divisive rhetoric seems to work. Too many of the most politically active folks in both parties I talk with across Washington, are motivated by anger, fear and demonizing others. Too many believe those who quietly pull people together and compromise are weak. From my experience in public office, the opposite is true. Some of the squishiest or laziest elected officials are those who loudly divide people and refuse to compromise.

Some of their supporters think that’s courageous, but it’s not. Courage is entering the arena already knowing what criticism will come. Only those tough and courageous enough to take arrows from both their own and the other side, listen, then quietly pull people together. Negotiating with opponents while defending one’s core values, taking a position you know will tick-off some friends and supporters, takes guts, requires conviction, isn’t for the squishy or lazy. Pulling people together is a democratic community’s glue. Those who pull people together and build community are real leaders.

But enough seriousness. It’s sunny. Let’s get outside and play.

---
| |

name: Governor Ralph Northam
subject: Flip the legislature for gun safety
disclaimer_text: Paid for by The Way Ahead The Way Ahead
body:
We can flip the General Assembly this November and pass the gun safety laws Virginians deserve.
| | | [](https://act.ralphnortham.com/page/m/296f21c1/39260142/42a46953/62639971/2166445256/VEsH/?g=0cliBlH8x8VbPwb7zJ42YiA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/twa19")
---
Friends-- Once again, dozens of lives have been shattered by gun violence. Pam and I grieve with the communities and the families of those killed and injured in El Paso and Dayton. Two months ago, after twelve people were killed and four people were injured in a mass shooting in Virginia Beach, I called a special session of the General Assembly because gun violence is an emergency, felt across our communities daily. I asked legislators for more than just their thoughts and prayers. I asked them for votes and laws -- votes to pass commonsense gun legislation, laws that will keep gun violence from killing more innocent people in our schools, office buildings, churches, stores, movie theaters, and synagogues. Our proposals were so commensense that this week even the President of the United States proposed passing some of them, such as background checks and an extreme risk protective order. Yet Republicans in the General Assembly refused to even come to the table, leaving the special session after just 90 minutes. Enough is enough. The time to save lives is now. I've said it before, and I'll say it again: if we can't change their minds, let's change their seats. **[Chip in $10 or more today so we can flip the General Assembly this November and pass the gun safety laws Virginians deserve.](https://act.ralphnortham.com/page/m/296f21c1/39260142/42a46953/62639970/2166445256/VEsE/?g=0cliBlH8x8VbPwb7zJ42YiA "http://secure.actblue.com/donate/vamajority2019#")** Take care, Ralph Northam

body:

| | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/8856758/86372209/1729145530?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDcvMS83NDI2NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzYjIwMDkyNy05NmI4LWU5MTEtYmNkMC0yODE4NzgzOTFlZmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Z4BGLreWnP7kwsrNTevGbDBv4iPZ6__0fs94MF1VszA=&refcode=emheader080619) | | Friend,

The chaotic gubernatorial election in Georgia last year was a result of a much larger plan – stifling the voices of the young, poor, and people of color at the ballot box.

In the last decade, a conservative Supreme Court majority has decided the following:
* **Citizens United v. FEC, 2010** : Corporations are people and should be allowed to spend unlimited sums of money to influence elections.
* **Shelby County v. Holder, 2013** : States with a history of slavery, segregation and Jim Crow laws no longer need federal clearance before enacting new voting laws.
* **Rucho v. Common Cause, 2019** : Federal courts have no role in reining in partisan gerrymandering.

[**We need a Senate that will overturn Citizens United, restore the Voting Rights Act, and guarantee equal representation for all Americans. Add your name if you agree.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/8856759/86372211/-413455148?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDcvMS83NDI2NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzYjIwMDkyNy05NmI4LWU5MTEtYmNkMC0yODE4NzgzOTFlZmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Z4BGLreWnP7kwsrNTevGbDBv4iPZ6__0fs94MF1VszA=&emci=c33528ec-95b8-e911-bcd0-281878391efb&emdi=3b200927-96b8-e911-bcd0-281878391efb&ceid=463701&contactdata=j5KfHTwRYy3UXtFbKNmO1Zhg1gmAi9nIt3xIV9IFPiH70%2Bw3icWjXvAVJ0eUlG%2BJAg71bcKwm1ksgb%2Fz%2FRg4PbqPAoVbZFc%2BJwYzNdQPytQ%3D&refcode=em080619a&refcode2=3b200927-96b8-e911-bcd0-281878391efb)

Our election in Georgia doesn’t just affect the presidential or Senate elections, but also our state legislature that passed this year’s horrific abortion ban which was praised by Mike Pence when he visited our state last week.

Our democracy – that millions of people have given their lives to defend – isn’t working for us, and you can help me change that. Shifting the balance of power on the Supreme Court begins with a Democratic Senate majority and that starts right here in Georgia.

**[Add your name to stand with me and help defend voting rights for all Americans – I can’t do it without you.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/8856761/86372215/-413455148?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDcvMS83NDI2NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzYjIwMDkyNy05NmI4LWU5MTEtYmNkMC0yODE4NzgzOTFlZmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Z4BGLreWnP7kwsrNTevGbDBv4iPZ6__0fs94MF1VszA=&emci=c33528ec-95b8-e911-bcd0-281878391efb&emdi=3b200927-96b8-e911-bcd0-281878391efb&ceid=463701&contactdata=j5KfHTwRYy3UXtFbKNmO1Zhg1gmAi9nIt3xIV9IFPiH70%2Bw3icWjXvAVJ0eUlG%2BJAg71bcKwm1ksgb%2Fz%2FRg4PbqPAoVbZFc%2BJwYzNdQPytQ%3D&refcode=em080619a&refcode2=3b200927-96b8-e911-bcd0-281878391efb)**

body:
I'm asking for 50,000 signatures to demand McConnell cut the Senate's vacation short and return to work IMMEDIATELY to pass Democrats' critical gun safety legislation. Their inaction has cost us too m
---
| | I refuse to wait a second longer. [_**I 'm calling on Mitch McConnell to bring the Senate back from their vacation NOW and pass Democrats' emergency gun safety legislation. Will you sign your name next to mine before midnight? >>**_](https://secure.benrlujan.com/page/m/40f6b4f4/18e32a7f/5492eedb/7bccc604/2335677473/VEsH/?g=YeDMcDpTVzRcsCgKwv9UzQ "https://secure.benrlujan.com/page/s/gun-safety-survey?source=20190806_em_pet_b1_lujan_all") When I learned of the horrific mass shootings and acts of domestic terrorism in El Paso and Dayton, I was heartbroken. But I was also angry. Because I've spent the last 3 months watching Mitch McConnell block House Democrats' critical gun safety legislation. Legislation I helped bring to the House floor for a vote. Legislation I was proud to support. Legislation that will save lives. But instead of acting, I've watched Mitch McConnell do everything in his power to keep our gun safety package from getting a vote. I watched him and his Republican Senate leave for a month-long vacation. Then, I watched tragedy strike. **And I knew at that very second -- sitting on Mitch McConnell 's desk -- was a bill that could save lives.** It makes me sick to think of McConnell and his Republican Senate on vacation while Americans are dying from gun violence. [_**I 'm asking for 50,000 signatures to demand McConnell cut the Senate's vacation short and return to work IMMEDIATELY to pass Democrats' critical gun safety legislation. Their inaction has cost us too many lives. >>**_](https://secure.benrlujan.com/page/m/40f6b4f4/18e32a7f/5492eedb/7bccc604/2335677473/VEsE/?g=YeDMcDpTVzRcsCgKwv9UzQ "https://secure.benrlujan.com/page/s/gun-safety-survey?source=20190806_em_pet_b1_lujan_all") Our nation is still grieving, we're still healing from wounds that may never close. But every second Republicans wait to prevent future tragedies like the ones we just witnessed is a gross miscarriage of justice. They need to return to work. I need your voice alongside mine to demand it. Thank you, Ben Ray Lujan
---

name: Mary Mancini
subject: last weekend
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY THE TENNESSEE DEMOCRATIC PARTY, DR
body:
Peter,

Saturday morning, someone walked into a mall in El Paso and started shooting. 20 dead 26 injured. Less than 24 hours later someone walked into a bar in Dayton and started shooting. 9 dead 27 injured.

Virginia Tech. Sandy Hook. Orlando. Las Vegas.

32. 27. 49. 58.

We are beyond crisis.

American children are afraid of school. Not because of bullying, or worrying about a math test. They’re afraid of being shot.

I want to say “Don’t worry. You’re safe. That won’t happen to you. Shootings aren’t normal.” But I can't. There have been over 17 mass shootings this year in America. It’s August. We’re not safe. This could happen to you. Shootings are normal.

We have a serious problem and we need to fix it. It’s not a Republican or Democrat problem. It’s an American problem. And it’s a problem that has a solution. Over 84% of Tennesseans are in favor of universal background checks – but the only checks our Republican leaders care about are the ones they’re getting from the NRA and gun lobby. Congressmen Green, Kustoff, Desjarlais, Roe, Rose, Fleishmann and Burchett all voted against the house background checks bill.

We deserve better. We deserve safety. We deserve action.

Evil exists. There is nothing we can do about it. But good also exists. And so does sanity. And when you put the two together you get common sense gun laws. The culture of corruption fueled by the hateful, racist, and violent rhetoric of the GOP leadership has no place in Tennessee, yet alone America.

We need to elect good. We need to elect sane. We need to elect leaders that aren’t afraid of the NRA. Leaders who are capable of stepping up and doing something about the mass shooting epidemic in this country. We simply can’t afford not to.

Mary

--

Mary Mancini

Chair, Tennessee Democratic Party

Team,
As our wildfire seasons get longer and more destructive here in Montana and other western states, there is no question that climate change is posing serious risks to our communities.
Right now, our brave firefighters are putting out flames in at least a dozen active wildfires across the state. These fires, like the Beeskove Fire near Missoula and the North Hills Fire near Helena, are threatening our homes, ranches, and our pristine environment and recreation areas.
Montanans need a Senator who will do everything in their power to protect our state from these wildfires, including addressing the threat of the climate crisis with the urgency it deserves.
[**Unlike Steve Daines, I 'll fight to address climate change when I'm in the Senate. subject: President Trump and First Lady to visit Dayton and El Paso tomorrow
body:
“Our hearts are shattered for every family whose parents, children, husbands, and wives were ripped from their arms and their lives.” | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=dd3bc2e1f4&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| The White House • August 6, 2019
---
| | |
# **President Trump and First Lady to visit Dayton and El Paso tomorrow**

**President Donald J. Trump** and First Lady Melania Trump will travel tomorrow to Dayton, Ohio, and El Paso, Texas, where mass shootings over the weekend claimed more than 30 lives and injured dozens more.

**“We are outraged** and sickened by this monstrous evil, the cruelty, the hatred, the malice, the bloodshed, and the terror,” President Trump said yesterday at the White House. “Our hearts are shattered for every family whose parents, children, husbands, and wives were ripped from their arms and their lives.”

**The President** also called for bipartisan action to confront gun violence, condemned the scourge of white supremacy, and thanked law enforcement in Ohio and Texas “who responded to these atrocities with the extraordinary grace and courage of American heroes.”

** ** [Watch: “We must honor the sacred memory of those we have lost.”](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=75d1a67ff8&e=3ee4985eea)

**Read** : [The President’s full remarks on the shootings in Texas and Ohio](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=41b5a7b1a5&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
|
## **America takes historic action to cut off the dictatorship in Venezuela**

**Nicolas Maduro** and his cronies in Venezuela have illegitimately usurped power, abused human rights, arbitrarily arrested and detained Venezuelan citizens, curtailed the freedom of the press, and continually undermined Venezuela’s democratically elected National Assembly and its leader, Interim President Juan Guaido.

**Today** , however, the Maduro dictatorship woke up to a new reality in Venezuela.

**With executive action** yesterday, President Trump imposed a sweeping set of sanctions that blocks all property of the Venezuelan Government in the jurisdiction of the United States. This historic move is the first action of its kind from the United States against a government in the Western Hemisphere in more than 30 years.

**President Trump** will continue to cut off the financial resources of Maduro and his enablers, which include officials in Russia and China. The Trump Administration has issued 30 rounds of sanctions targeting approximately 200 individuals and entities, focused on corruption networks that steal from the suffering Venezuelan people.

**America stands** with Venezuelans as they continue to fight for freedom, human rights, democracy, and the rule of law in their country. President Trump maintains strong support for Interim President Guaido and the democratically elected National Assembly. While sanctioning Maduro strongly, the United States has taken careful measures to provide the Venezuelan people with access to humanitarian aid, including food and medicine.

**“All options** are on the table,” White House Press Secretary Stephanie Grisham said in a statement. “The Maduro dictatorship must end for Venezuela to have a stable, democratic, and prosperous future—free from the horrors of socialism that have ravaged this once great country.”

**National Security Advisor** John Bolton added that**“** with this E.O., all those individuals that continue to support Maduro must make a choice. Stick with a sinking dictatorship or move toward the right side of history!”

[President Trump is cutting off the resources of Maduro and his cronies.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=6dae3c123e&e=3ee4985eea)

**Press Secretary Grisham:** [Full statement on the President’s order ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=ee6d7539f3&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
|

name: Beto O'Rourke
subject: El Paso
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY BETO FOR AMERICA
body:
I am so proud of my hometown of El Paso. Always have been.

I tell our story wherever I go. This place of immigrants, of people from
all over the planet, who came here to do better for themselves and to do
better for this country. I tell people about how we are one of the safest
cities in the United States. Nearly 700,000 people and we've averaged only
18 murders a year.

And I make sure that people know that those two things are connected. It
is the very presence of immigrants and asylum seekers and refugees that
has made us so safe. We don't just tolerate our differences, we embrace
them. We treat each other with the dignity and respect we are owed as
human beings. It is the foundation of our success and our safety.

I've always thought the example set by El Paso could offer a path forward
for a country that is so consumed by our differences and our divisions.

Si queremos asegurar nuestro país, I often say, necesitamos seguir el
ejemplo de El Paso.

But on Saturday, we realized that we can take no comfort in our safety, in
our ability to see the best in each other by seeing ourselves in one
another. That, as Martin Luther King Jr. said, "we are caught in an
inescapable network of mutuality, tied in a single garment of destiny."

Though El Paso is a safe community, we are part of a country that is
violent. A country that has failed to adopt laws that would allow us to
perform a background check on everyone who wants to own a firearm. One
that still allows weapons designed for war to be sold into our
communities. We lost 40,000 of our fellow Americans to gun violence last
year — inexplicable but for the stranglehold that the gun lobby has on
Congress and the White House, and the fear that our elected
representatives have of the NRA.

And though we are a city that prides itself as a home of immigrants, we
live in America at a moment that the President seeks to make us afraid of
immigrants, to see them as animals and rapists and killers, a threat to
our very lives. An invasion that must be stopped. An infestation that must
be stamped out.

[1]El Paso vigil

At a rally in Florida in May, President Trump asked how America could stop
immigrants from coming into the country.

"Shoot them!" someone yelled back.

As the crowd roared their approval, the President smiled.

That violence, that hatred, that fear found us on Saturday. Drove more
than 600 miles to a community that is 85% Mexican-American. A community of
first- and second-generation immigrants. It walked into one of the busiest
Walmarts in the country, full of families from El Paso and our sister city
of Ciudad Juárez and killed 22 people. A 90-year old man shot dead next to
the wife he'd been married to for 70 years. A 15 year old boy about to
start his sophomore year in high school. Young parents, both of them
murdered, as they shielded their 2-month old son.

Death and suffering. Pain and devastation. Families grieving an
indescribable loss.

But this terrorist, echoing the words of Donald Trump and hosts on Fox
News in his manifesto, will fail to achieve his aims of stopping America
from being America. A country of immigrants, asylum seekers and refugees.
A country which 243 years ago chose to define itself not along lines of
race or ethnicity but instead on the principle that we are all created
equal.

This hatred and violence won't define us. The people I've met at
University Medical Center and Del Sol Hospital, shot in the chest, in the
stomach, in the back, in the leg, in the arm, in the foot, all of them
meeting their pain with courage will do that. All of them recovering as
they receive the care and help they need from their fellow El Pasoans.
Lines around the block at blood donation centers. Vigils of thousands
throughout the community, in Central El Paso, on the eastside, and over in
Horizon.

Though on Saturday, El Paso bore the brunt of the hatred and violence in
this country, I believe our community also holds some of the answers. Not
just to our pain and challenges, but to those of the country.

Because we now know first hand that no physical distance, no set of
circumstances unique to your community, can separate you from what is
happening to all of us in this country. We are all in this together.
Unless we make it harder for people to kill, unless we stop this racism
and fear, unless we stop seeing our differences as dangerous, unless we
hold those in the highest positions of public trust accountable — this
violence will find every one of us, sooner or later.

It is on all of us to stand up and be counted, especially when we feel the
consequences of America at its worst, to fight for this country to be its
best.

That is the spirit of El Paso, a city where everyone belongs. And the hope
I have for America is that we become a country like that too.

- Beto

name: Dick Durbin
subject: Enough is enough
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Dick Durbin
body:
#
[**We need to take a stand, Peter. Will you contribute $5 to support the eventual Democratic nominees taking on NRA-backed Republican Senators?**](http://action.dickdurbin.?g=ofPncVH8x8VcsCmaF7wmZzA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/dd-enough-is-enough?refcode=em190806&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=34223&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&addr1=&addr2=&city=Englewood&state=FL&phone=&employer=&occupation=")
Peter,
Enough is enough.
We have had enough of living in constant fear of gun violence, of waking up to news of our fellow Americans being gunned down, of losing friends and family members in mass shootings in our schools, shopping centers, movie theaters, concert venues, and places of worship.
And we have had enough of Republican politicians standing with the gun lobby and the NRA instead of taking action on commonsense gun reform to save countless American lives.
**We can 't wait for the GOP to act any longer. The only way we are going to pass gun reform is by defeating NRA-backed Senators in swing states and taking back the Senate.**
[**Peter, now is the time to take a stand. Contribute $5 to the eventual Democratic nominees in Arizona, Iowa, and Kentucky.**](http://action.dickdurbin.?g=ofPncVH8x8VcsCmaF7wmZzA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/dd-enough-is-enough?refcode=em190806&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=34223&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&addr1=&addr2=&city=Englewood&state=FL&phone=&employer=&occupation=")
This weekend, a total of 31 people were murdered and dozens more injured during an act of domestic terrorism in an El Paso Walmart and a mass shooting in Dayton, Ohio.
In Chicago, a gunman wounded seven people near a playground on the city's West Side.
And these are just the most recent examples. In 2019, we've already experienced 251 mass shootings--that's more shootings than days in the year. We must stop this epidemic.
[**Enough is enough, Peter. Can we count on you to help defeat NRA-backed Republicans and elect Democratic Senators who will fight for gun reform? Contribute $5 to help fund the eventual nominees in these crucial swing states.**](http://action.dickdurbin.?g=ofPncVH8x8VcsCmaF7wmZzA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/dd-enough-is-enough?refcode=em190806&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=34223&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&addr1=&addr2=&city=Englewood&state=FL&phone=&employer=&occupation=")
My best,
Dick Durbin
